docs.title: LOCO School
docs.text: This is a website for LOCO School documentation.

docs.title: locoboard API reference
docs.text:
turn_led_on turn on an LED

turn_led_off turn off an LED

docs.title: Useful links
docs.text: STEMFIE 3D-printable construction set

docs.title: Topics to cover
docs.text:
Not necessarily in this order

  • Algorithms (recap)
  • Basic syntax of C/Python (function names, parameters)
  • Compilation & flashing
  • Board hardware overview
  • Board API
  • Variables & data types
  • Conditions (if/else)
  • Loops (for/while)
  • Functions
  • Debugging (serial print)
  • Using external libraries
  • 3D design & printing?

docs.title: Sessions
docs.text:
• Session 1
  • Session 2

docs.title: Goals in getting to know the robot
Goals in working with a group of children
Goals
docs.text: • Refresh basic algorithmic concepts

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
• Basic commands
  • Step-by-step execution
  • Execution control: loops and conditions
  • Wrapping repetitive steps into functions

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: • some interactive task that we can solve together as a game?

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Get to know Arduino IDE
  • Learn to compile a program
  • Learn to upload the program to the board

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
• UI overview
  • The concept of compilation (convert words to machine-executable code)
  • How to compile in Arduino IDE
  • Error messages
  • How to connect and configure the board
  • How to upload a compiled program to the board

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: • Live demo (we show, they repeat)

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Internalize the fact that errors are natural
  • Understand the importance of debugging
  • Learn basics debugging techniques

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
• Discuss that we all make mistakes and there is no way around it
  • Basic debugging principle: comparing what we expect with what we get
  • Show how to examine the program state (serial print)
  • Demonstrate and discuss the importance of minimal examples

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Interactive demo and discussion

Task 1

Print some intermediate output to console

Task 2

Find and fix a bug in a given program

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Get to know the board components
  • Learn do-s and dont-s

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
• Show vanilla Arduino
  • Recap the concept of GPIO pins
  • Discuss why having an expansion shield makes life easier
  • Go over the board parts and discuss their functions
  • Show peripherals that we can connect to the board
  • Discuss basic electric safety
  • Discuss how to connect other devices (for which there is no interface on the board)

docs.title: Activities
docs.text: • Interactive discussion

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Understand the concept of a programming library
  • Get familiar with the principle of using the locoboard API
  • Get an idea about how to use what's not in the library

docs.title: Content
docs.text:
• Explain what a library is and how to interact with it
  • Show locoboard documentation
  • Discuss how to work with the documentation
  • Make an example on how to control some element (e.g., an LED)

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
• Interactive demo/discussion

Task 1

Write a program that shows something on the built-in LED matrix

Task 2

Complement it with some sound effects
